snippets: We remain focused on disciplined profitable growth in selected geographic areas such as North America, where our scale and strong manufacturing footprint provides advantages to our customers.
interest in adding incremental gas capacity is growing. Customers are focused on how capacity additions this decade can be decarbonized in the next decade
We are accelerating our focus and our strategy working with our supply chain partners on how to potentially create incremental capacity to meet this growing demand.
we booked eight HA gas turbine orders, four more than the same quarter last year and equal to HA orders in the full-year of 2023
many of them are going to have incremental gas capacity additions
we are bullish on the capacity addition, optionality or opportunity ahead for us with our utility customers
All three segments benefited from positive price in the quarter.
EBITDA margins expanded 470 basis points year-over-year...driven by price, productivity and volume.
Electrification is the one where we have the best opportunity to challenge ourselves on both growth and margins...given this segment has the strongest demand and pricing dynamics.
Wind EBITDA margins improved 400 basis points versus the prior year from continued cost reductions and positive price.
we will remain highly selective on new orders...only going to add to that backlog with materially different economic terms...price, other terms
Orders reached $9.7 billion and were approximately 1.3x first quarter revenue further expanding our backlog to $116 billion.
Our backlog continued to grow up over $8 billion compared to Q1 '23 with healthy margins.
Equipment backlog in this segment is up $6 billion compared to the first quarter of 2023 with healthy margins.
equipment margin and backlog remained healthy.
this strong orders and backlog that's coming into electrification will be something that supports margin expansion on those higher backlogs and margin as we move out past the 2025 timeframe.

key_takeaways: GE Vernova delivered a strong Q1 2024 debut as a public company with 5% organic revenue growth, ~470 bps EBITDA margin expansion, and backlog growing to $116B, reaffirming full-year 2024 guidance of $34B-$35B revenue and $700M-$1.1B free cash flow.

Gas Power demand is accelerating sharply—eight HA turbine orders in Q1 alone (equal to all of 2023), services orders up double-digits, and the company is actively evaluating supply chain capacity expansions to meet surging demand driven by AI/data center load growth and coal-to-gas switching.

Electrification is the fastest-growing and most favorably positioned segment, with orders at 2x+ revenue, backlog up $6B YoY, and 600 bps EBITDA margin expansion, while Wind continues its turnaround with onshore EBITDA positive for a third consecutive quarter.
risk_flags: G&A costs increased YoY due to public company standup and costs transferred from GE corporate, with management acknowledging this is an area of concern requiring urgent action.

Free cash flow was a net outflow of $661M in Q1, driven by ~$500M working capital outflow from inventory build and GE payable settlements, requiring significant H2 acceleration to meet full-year guidance.
Offshore wind remains loss-generating and highly selective on new orders; the segment is only expected to 'approach profitability' for full-year 2024, with execution risk on existing backlog.
Onshore wind order timing remains uncertain as U.S. developers navigate permitting challenges, creating revenue visibility risk for H2 ramp.
Gas Power supply chain capacity constraints may limit ability to capture accelerating equipment demand; management flagged need to work with supply chain partners on incremental capacity.
